description: The figure displays a ball-and-stick crystal structure model representing the B2 ordered cubic phase (space group Pm-3m, CsCl-type) of the Fe–Mn–Al alloy system studied via first-principles DFT calculations. The visualization shows a multi-cell arrangement with spheres of different colors (pink, purple, blue, and green) located at distinct crystallographic sites, including corner and body-center positions characteristic of the CsCl-type structure. The accompanying text summary reports that this atomic configuration serves as the computational model for the Fe_{1-x}Mn_xAl series ranging from FeAl to MnAl, where Al occupies one sublattice while Fe and Mn share the other. Lattice parameters reportedly increase monotonically from 0.570 nm for FeAl to 0.584 nm for MnAl as Mn content increases. The image provides the structural foundation for calculating formation enthalpy, electronic density of states, elastic constants, and mechanical properties, though the specific color-to-element assignments are not explicitly labeled within the figure itself.
